20251223_01.alt: A plate of 보쌈 (bossam), Korean pork belly lettuce wraps, with a snowy backdrop
20251223_01.caption: 보쌈 and snow - my first time making this

20251223_02.alt: Pan of lasagne cooling on the stove
20251223_02.caption: I don't have a big baking pan at home so it was nice to be able to make a big suburban lasagne

20251225_03.alt: Bowl of congee with scallions and ramen egg with Lao Gan Ma and white pepper in the background
20251225_03.caption: Christmas morning congee - also my first time making at home. I made the pork bone broth and ramen eggs the day before (I know I'm mixing cultures, but turns out it's hard to find century eggs in the Catskills).

20251225_04.alt: Chopping board with scallions and an empty jar labeled "PORK BONE BROTH" with blue painters tape
20251225_04.caption: Congee prep

20251225_05.alt: Serving bowls and plates with braised Napa cabbage, lions head meatballs, and mashed potatoes
20251225_05.caption: Christmas dinner was lions head meatballs, braised Napa cabbage, and mashed potatoes

20251228_06.alt: Kimchi grilled cheese sandwich with snowy woods in the background
20251228_06.caption: Kimchi grilled cheese sandwich

20251229_07.alt: Scones cooling on parchment paper, with a snowy backyard visible in the background
20251229_07.caption: I'm not a big baker but had some blueberries that were a little sad, not inedible, but not plump enough to eat fresh, so I baked them into these scones

20251229_08.alt: Plate of bbq with brisket, pickle chips, pickled banana peppers, potato salad, corn bread, and baked beans
20251229_08.caption: BBQ brisket and sides - I used the Crock Pot to slow cook the brisket all day which was very homey. I made the potato salad and baked beans from scratch too (turns out baked beans are tasty because they have a lot of brown sugar and molasses... indulgent)

20251230_09.alt: Two hands holding a plate with a brisket sandwich
20251230_09.caption: Brisket sandwich using leftovers from BBQ nite

20251231_10.alt: Chicken pot pie in a pan cooling on the stove
20251231_10.caption: Doesn't look too exciting but it's chicken pot pie. Another suburban comfort food that I rarely make in the city.

20260101_11.alt: Bowl of soup with croutons and 고춧가루 (gochugaru)
20260101_11.caption: Can't remember exactly what this was... I think it was a bean soup I had made the day before and then I made some croutons out of old bread that were a hit. 고춧가루 (gochugaru) and cheese on top.

20260102_12.alt: A pan of spices including star anise, cinnamon sticks, fennel, cloves, and others on the stovetop
20260102_12.caption: Toasting spices for phở broth. Going for a nature walk in the snow with a Thermos of phở broth is a top tier experience.

20260103_13.alt: A bowl of cubed Korean radish with spices for 깍두기 (kkakdugi, radish kimchi)
20260103_13.caption: 깍두기 (radish kimchi) in progress

20260103_14.alt: Dutch oven on the stove with corned beef, carrots, celery, onions
20260103_14.caption: I brined a brisket to make corned beef. I wanted to use the smoker to make pastrami, but after 5 days of brining, I was kind of just ready to eat lol

20260104_15.alt: Plate in the sun with corned beef hash and an egg on top
20260104_15.caption: Corned beef hash with my homemade corned beef

title: Homestead food prep
blurb: Wintering upstate and being away from the city makes me want to get cozy and make a bunch of indulgent meals from scratch. I could do a better job of documenting the food I make but honestly I usually just want to make and eat it without having to pick up a phone or camera every two minutes 😅. Here are some of the things I made this winter that I remembered to take a picture of.
subtitle: Seriously can't believe I corned my own beef
